name: Cambridge CSD3
funder: University of Cambridge / EPSRC / partner projects
oneLiner: Cambridge's broad CPU and A100 GPU service, linking local research compute with Dawn and the EIDF.
summary: CSD3 is Cambridge's shared service for data-driven discovery and hosts a wide range of CPU and GPU sub-clusters. The AI-relevant estate includes the Wilkes3 A100 GPU partition and several CPU partitions based on Cascade Lake, Ice Lake, and Sapphire Rapids systems, including high-bandwidth-memory nodes reserved for UKAEA projects. This breadth makes CSD3 a bridge between local institutional compute and larger national platforms such as Dawn and the EIDF GPU service.

keyFacts.hardware: Wilkes3 A100 GPU nodes plus Cascade Lake, Ice Lake, Sapphire Rapids, and Sapphire Rapids HBM CPU partitions
keyFacts.performance: Around 30,412 CPU cores and 746,496 GPU cores with 8 PB storage
keyFacts.funding: University and project-backed shared research service
keyFacts.energy: Shared regional platform spanning multiple hardware generations

access.route: Accessible to academic and industrial users through Cambridge Research Computing and EPSRC-backed allocations.

useCases: Preprocessing and postprocessing on large CPU partitions
Hybrid workflows linked to Dawn and EIDF resources

sources.label: CSD3 documentation - Sapphire Rapids HBM nodes
